title: Coherent spin waves in a maximal entropy phase
authors: Arnau Romaguera, Eugenio Paris, Elizabeth Skoropata, Stefano Agrestini, Mirian Garcia-Fernandez, Marisa Medarde, Noah Schnitzer, Lopa Bhatt, Berit H. Goodge, Yun Yen, Matthias Krack, Michael Schüler, Romain Sibille, Tom Fennell, Daniel G. Mazzone, Jakob Lass, Ellen Fogh, Anirudha Ghosh, Marco Caputo, Carlos William Galdino, Zhijia Zhang, Thorsten Schmitt, Milan Radovic, Luc Patthey, Hiroki Ueda, Monica Ciomaga Hatnean, Elia Razzoli
abstract: In solids, disorder is conventionally regarded as detrimental to coherence. It typically localizes and dampens collective excitations, as exemplified by Anderson localization or the broadening of magnetic modes in systems lacking long-range order. While high-entropy materials are specifically designed to harness disorder and stabilize homogeneous mixed-phase structures that can display unique properties, this same disorder is nonetheless expected to preclude the formation of coherent magnetic excitations. To test the limits of this picture, we selected the antiferromagnetic system YBaCuFeO5, as it features two distinct transition metal atoms with significantly different magnetic moments, rendering its spin dynamics exceptionally sensitive to local atomic ordering. Combining resonant inelastic x-ray scattering and linear spin wave theory, we reveal a surprising paradox: YBaCuFeO5 exhibits an unexpected, entropy-driven mixed phase, in which disorder, rather than reducing the lifetime of the collective excitations, favors coherence. In this mixed phase, the spin waves remain dispersive, markedly distinct from those expected for an ordered ground state, and exhibit well-defined acoustic and optical branches separated by a large optical gap. These results demonstrate that in entropy-stabilized magnets, disorder can favor coherent collective modes previously thought to be exclusive to low-entropy systems.
summary: This paper explores the magnetic dynamics of the high-entropy material YBaCuFeO5. Contrary to the expectation that disorder destroys coherence, the authors demonstrate that an entropy-stabilized mixed phase of Fe and Cu atoms supports well-defined, underdamped spin waves. By using RIXS and spin dynamics simulations, they show that this disordered state possesses a large optical gap and robust collective modes. This finding suggests new strategies for engineering disordered materials to host stable, high-energy magnetic excitations.
main_problem: Investigating whether atomic disorder in high-entropy materials necessarily degrades the coherence of collective magnetic excitations, specifically in the YBaCuFeO5 system.
main_result: The study reveals a paradox where disorder in an entropy-driven mixed phase actually favors the formation of coherent, underdamped acoustic and optical spin-wave branches with a large optical gap.
method: The researchers combined Resonant Inelastic X-ray Scattering (RIXS) at the Cu and Fe L3 edges with theoretical modeling using Linear Spin Wave Theory (LSWT), Atomistic Spin Dynamics (ASD), and DFT+U.
model_or_system: The physical system is YBaCuFeO5 (YBCFO), an antiferromagnetic layered compound featuring a maximal entropy mixed phase where Fe and Cu atoms are disordered within the ab-planes.
key_observables: Dynamically spin structure factors S(q, omega), magnon dispersion omega(q), optical spin gap, and linewidths (damping).
important_parameters: Exchange couplings (J_ab, J_perp), spin quantum numbers, configurational entropy, and the transition temperatures T_CM and T_S.
main_assumptions: The use of the Heisenberg model and the approximation of the spiral phase using a weak ferromagnetic next-nearest-neighbor interaction.
figures_summary: Figure 1 shows the crystal structure and RIXS intensity; Figure 2 compares LSWT results for different atomic orderings; Figure 3 shows spin-wave evolution as a function of disorder; Figure 4 compares experimental vs. simulated peak energies and intensities.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the problem of disorder-induced damping, describes the YBCFO system, presents RIXSS experimental data, compares various atomic ordering models (monoatomic, checkerboard, mixed), and concludes with the identification of the entropy-stabilized mixed phase as the source of coherent modes.
why_interesting: It challenges the conventional intuition that disorder leads to localization and damping, providing a mechanism where entropy-driven disorder can actually stabilize coherent many-body excitations.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: Fig. 1 RIXS measurements at Fe and Cu L3 edges at 20 K. a, Crystal structure of YBCFO generated using VESTA [16], with the orientation of the tetragonal unit-cell axes indicated (Cu in blue, Fe in brown). b, Experimental RIXS intensity at Cu L3-edge, integrated along high-symmetry path shown in the inset of c. c, d, Experimental S(q, ω) at Cu and Fe L3 edges, respectively, after correction for self-absorption and the geometric spin-flip factor. Circles mark the extracted dispersion. e, RIXS intensity at Fe L3-edge, integrated along the same high-symmetry path.

Fig. 2 LSWT calculations for two atomic-ordering configurations in YBCFO. a, Atomic configuration containing staggered monoatomic Fe and Cu planes (Cu in blue, Fe in brown). b–d, LSWT results for the configuration in a, showing the Cu- and Fe- projected S(q, ω), respectively, along high symmetry lines connecting Γ = (0, 0, 0), P = (0.25, 0.25, 0), X = (0.5, 0, 0), Z = (0, 0, 0.5), P′ = (0.25, 0.25, 0.5) and X’ = (0.5, 0, 0.5) (r.l.u. in units of 2π/a, 2π/b, 2π/c). Thin gray lines indicate the LSWT eigenvalues. e, Atomic configuration with diatomic Fe and Cu planes in a checkerboard arrangement. f–h, LSWT results for the configuration in e. d,h, Total and atomically projected dynamical spin...

Fig. 3 Entropy dependence of the magnon optical-branch dispersion in YBCFO. a, ASD SCu(q, ω) calculations as a function of the local ordering in the 2D planes ⟨dS⟩. Representative configurations of the 2D plane ordering for the ordered (⟨dS⟩= 0, 2) and high entropy (⟨dS⟩= 1) phases are shown at the bottom, as schematics of the atomic and spin configurations (Cu in blue, Fe in brown). b–e, Properties of YBCFO as a function of ⟨dS⟩: Configuration entropy Sconf (b), spin optical gap at Γ and spin-waves energy at P (c), normalized intensity of the optical branch (d), and lattice and magnetic Bragg reflection intensities (e). Dashed lines in c–e represent the experimental values. Shaded areas...

Fig. 4 Experimental and simulated momentum dependence of magnon peak parame- ters. a, Undamped mode energies ω0 extracted from DHO fits to the experimental RIXS spectra, compared with the simulated peak positions for the high-entropy configuration. b,c, Integrated inten- sities obtained from numerical integration of the fitted DHO component for the Cu and Fe L3 edges, respectively, compared with the simulated S(q, ω) projected onto Cu and Fe atoms. d,e, Experimen- tal linewidths 2γ (approximately the peak full width at half maximum) extracted from the DHO fits, compared with calculations for undamped (UD) and locally damped (LD) dynamics. Blue and brown symbols denote values extracted from...

Fig. 4 shows that the mixed phase model ASD calculations accurately reproduce experimental dispersions, intensities, and damping. For the optical branch, the exper- imental ratio is small, γ/ω ≤0.22 across all studied q-points, indicating that the optical magnons are underdamped, and thus coherent [22–24], which is striking given the significant influence of atomic disorder on the dispersion at Γ. In the acoustic branch, the experimental ratio γ/ω is larger, but remains within the underdamped limit (γ/ω ∼0.6 < 1).
